seoTitle: How to Create a New Airtable Base Easily
metaDescription: Build a new Airtable base from a blank canvas, template, or CSV import, then set up your first table and relational structure.
intro: Every Airtable project starts with a base, and setting one up right from the beginning — with clear field types and a plan for how tables will relate — saves you from restructuring later. This guide covers starting a blank base, customizing it, and laying out your first table and fields.

steps.heading: Open the main dashboard
steps.body: Navigate to your primary Airtable home dashboard where existing bases and workspaces are listed.

steps.heading: Create a new base
steps.body: Click Create and choose the blank base option, or start from a CSV import or pre-built template instead.

steps.heading: Customize the base details
steps.body: Set your base title, icon, and color theme to make it easy to identify among your other bases.

steps.heading: Structure your first table
steps.body: Rename the default column fields to match the data you actually want to track.

steps.heading: Add specialized field types
steps.body: Add fields like Single Select or Attachments as needed beyond basic text fields.

steps.heading: Prepare for relational structure
steps.body: Create a second table if your project needs connected data, setting the stage for linked records between tables.

faq.question: Should I start from a blank base or a template?
faq.answer: A template can save time if your use case matches an existing one closely; a blank base gives you full control if your structure is unique.

faq.question: Can I import a CSV directly into a new base?
faq.answer: Yes, when creating a base you can choose to import a CSV file, which automatically creates a table with matching fields.

faq.question: How many tables can one base have?
faq.answer: You can add multiple tables within a single base, which is how you build relational structures using linked records.

faq.question: Can I change the base's color and icon later?
faq.answer: Yes, the base title, icon, and color theme can be edited at any time from the base settings.
